kept.text:
Numbers talk…bullshit walks!

In 2025, Waymo is estimated to have generated $300M in revenue for… $5 billion in losses! (And overall, their cumulative losses are estimated at $20-30 billion).

Assuming their 3,000 autonomous taxis cost $200K each (which seems very high to me), this fleet would have cost them $600M — or, if aggressively amortized over 3 years, about $200M per year.

In other words, 95% of Waymo’s costs are not the vehicles themselves, but everything else: operating infrastructure, supervision, maintenance, roadside assistance, charging, cleaning, etc.

The scale of these costs is a clear sign of the complexity and importance of all the infrastructure that must be built to operate such a fleet.

That’s why we shouldn’t be surprised by how long it’s also taking Tesla to put all of this in place. It is, de facto, an extremely heavy undertaking — something Waymo’s actual costs demonstrate very concretely. 

If you’re surprised by how long it’s taking, it’s probably because  you don’t understand the operational constraints.

Tesla has the game-changing technology and hardware with the Cybercab and FSD, but just give them time to build out the operating infrastructure.

kept.text:
The EV industry has a dirty little secret: the "clean" batteries powering the revolution require massive, energy-guzzling drying ovens to manufacture. The holy grail is the dry electrode process, a method that scraps the ovens, shrinks the factory footprint, and slashes capital costs. 

But there is a reason it has been so incredibly hard to scale. Dry powder is a physical nightmare. It naturally clogs machinery, tears under pressure, and creates so much resistance that heavy steel calender rollers can literally destroy themselves.

Until now. 

A newly surfaced trio of patents reveals exactly how @Tesla is taming the physics of dry manufacturing. By isolating and breaking three distinct bottlenecks in the pipeline, they have engineered a continuous, high-speed production loop. 

Here is the mechanical blueprint of how they pulled it off.

🌬️ Patent US20260108943: Weaponizing the bridge for flawless powder flow

The first step in dry battery manufacturing is getting the raw powder to drop evenly onto the pressing rollers. The problem is that dry powders are notoriously sticky. They tend to interlock and form solid bridges, completely choking the flow of materials. Traditional mechanical gates only make this worse by creating pinch points that crush the delicate particles.

To solve this, Tesla designed a specialized hopper detailed in patent US 2026/0108943 A1. Instead of using moving mechanical parts to stop and start the powder, they use the bridging effect to their advantage. The invention features a wedge-shaped trough lined with porous side plates. By pumping precise pulses of compressed dry air through thousands of microscopic pinholes, the system fluidizes the powder, allowing it to glide smoothly like a liquid.

When the air is cut off, the powder naturally bridges together and halts instantly. This pulsing air acts as a friction-free, invisible brake. To ensure perfect distribution, a complex network of air channels sits behind the porous walls. This allows the system to pulse air into specific zones, fixing uneven powder drops on the fly without ever relying on physical gates that inevitably wear out.

🔬 Patent US20260121060: Shrinking the glue to eliminate structural craters

Once the powder successfully drops, it needs to hold together as a solid sheet. The problem here lies in the structural binders, which act as the glue holding the energy-storing materials together. Standard commercial binders are simply too large, typically measuring forty to seventy micrometers across (roughly the width of a human hair). When pressed by hot rollers, these bulky particles create uneven pressure points and are often ripped right out of the mixture, leaving destructive craters in the final battery film.

Tesla tackled this microscopic defect issue in patent US 2026/0121060 A1 by bringing the milling process in-house. The solution involves feeding the raw binder into a pressurized machine that blasts the material with focused air streams at high pressure (120 psi). A sorting wheel spinning at a blistering 8,000 revolutions per minute acts as a microscopic bouncer, flinging large chunks back into the grinding chamber.

Only particles pulverized down to exactly ten micrometers are allowed to exit. By shrinking the binder to match the size of the active materials, the surface area contact increases exponentially. This microscopic glue grips the surrounding powder so tightly that the hot rollers can no longer pluck it away. Tesla then blends this perfectly sized binder through a dual-path mixing process to protect the delicate structures, allowing the factory to print flawless, highly durable battery sheets at rapid speeds.

⚙️ Patent US20260121013: Levitating steel to hold a microscopic gap

With the powder flowing smoothly and the microscopic glue holding firm, the final hurdle is the actual pressing phase. To flatten the powder into a dense film, heavy steel rollers must apply an immense amount of crushing force. The problem is that this intense pressure causes the solid steel to bend slightly, ruining the microscopic accuracy of the film. Even worse, if the powder supply breaks, the spinning rollers can violently slam together and destroy the machine.

Tesla engineered a brilliant mechanical safety net to control this violence, outlined in patent US 2026/0121013 A1. The invention uses a dynamic system of hydraulic and magnetic actuators attached to the heavy steel blocks holding the rollers. Instead of just pushing the rollers together, this system can simultaneously push and pull on different support bearings. This choreographed tension acts much like a puppeteer pulling strings in opposite directions to keep a heavy object perfectly still, actively countering the bending of the solid steel.

To know exactly how much force to apply, a central processor relies on a mathematical model that fuses data from physical, optical, and electronic sensors. It calculates the real gap by taking the measured distance and mathematically subtracting the estimated bending in real time. This active balancing act maintains a flawless gap, as small as one micrometer, and creates an invisible safety net that completely prevents the rollers from crashing, even if the factory line runs entirely out of powder.

🚀 Forging the continuous dry manufacturing loop

When you step back and look at these three patents together, the grand strategy becomes crystal clear. Tesla is not just tweaking a chemistry recipe, they are shifting battery production from a slow and messy chemical art into a high-speed, continuous mechanical extrusion process.

The breathable hopper ensures the raw materials never stop flowing. The jet-milled binders ensure the film never tears under pressure. The levitating rollers ensure the heavy machinery never has to be halted for crashes or recalibrations.

By solving these three distinct physical bottlenecks, Tesla has unlocked the true commercial viability of the dry battery electrode. This synchronized mechanical pipeline is the foundation needed to churn out cheaper, safer, and massively energy-dense cells for the next generation of affordable vehicles and global power grids.

kept.text:
There are many effective ways to extend your Tesla’s battery life. Here are a few key tips taken directly from Tesla’s official manual:

1. Charge at Level 1 or Level 2 (Mobile Connectors or Wall Chargers) whenever possible. Save the Supercharging for road trips or long drives.

2. For vehicles with a recommended daily charge limit of 80%, keep your daily charge limit at about 80. Save 100% for those times when you've got a long drive ahead of you. See Charge Settings.

3. Charge more frequently. Waiting until the Battery is low to charge can strain the Battery over time.

4. Avoid leaving the Battery at or near 0% or 100% for long periods of time whenever possible.

5. When you store your Tesla for long periods of time, leave the Battery at around 50% charge and keep it plugged in if possible. Use the charge settings to maintain the 50% level.

6. 10. For LFP battery vehicles Tesla recommends setting your daily charge limit to 100% and charging to 100% at least once per week. This helps calibrate the battery management system.

He also held a massive ~$500M short position against $TSLA for years and Elon confronted him directly in 2022 and Gates admitted he still hadn’t closed it out… this costed him $ billions in losses as the stock soared and no one really knows till this day if he actually closed out his short positions. 

Now in 2026, the first Tesla Semi just rolled off the high-volume production line in Nevada with up to 500-mile range at full 82,000 lb load and real-world efficiency fleets like PepsiCo are already loving it and seeing the benefits of owning it. 

I kinda feel bad for this man for being so wrong tbh… but at the same time I wouldn’t really care if his short position against the most innovative company, blew up.

kept.text:
🔥🚀 Tesla Spring Update 2026.14.3 is now rolling out to the 4th batch of vehicles (reaching ~38% of the fleet)!

Only bug fixes are expected compared to 2026.14.1 and .2.
Notice anything new? Feel free to share!

2026.14.1 changes:
✅ Self-Driving App [AI4]
With the new Self-Driving app, subscribe with a single tap, learn how to activate and use the feature, and view ongoing stats.

✅ "Hey Grok"
Launch Grok by saying "Hey Grok." Go to Grok > Settings to enable. You can also set location based reminders, like "remind me to pick up milk when I'm near home." Say "goodbye" to dismiss.

✅ Pet Mode
Choose and name your pet by going to Controls > Display > Customize Pet Mode.

✅ Visual Updates [New Model 3/Y]
The center display now features higher quality images of your Tesla, and an enhanced park scene environment.

✅ Trips
Trips are now in the Energy app, where you can create and manage multiple trips to track energy usage across different drives, routes, or conditions. Go to App Launcher > Energy > Trips. For quick access to the Trips Card, swipe left on the Media Card.

✅ Sketchpad
Unleash your inner artist with the enhanced Sketchpad, now supporting stickers and emojis. Save your sketches to access them in the Mobile App and share.

✅ Custom Wraps and License Plates [S/X]
Model S & X owners can now personalize their Tesla avatar with window tints, custom wraps & license plates.

✅ Blind Spot Warning Accent Lights
Accent lights now turn red when an object is in your blind spot and your turn signal is engaged, or when an approaching object is detected while parked. To disable, go to Controls > Lights > Blind Spot Warning Accent Lights.

✅ Immersive Sound Upgrade [New Model 3 & Y w/ premium audio]
Premium Immersive Sound uses advanced sound extraction to place the listener in front of a detailed soundstage within an Immersive space. This enhancement works with all streaming sources.

✅ Music App Queuing
Swipe right on tracks in Apple Music or Spotify to add them to the queue. In Apple Music, tap and hold on a track to add or remove from Favorites. In Spotify, swipe left to add or remove from Liked Songs.

✅ Automatic Software Updates
Your Tesla can now automatically install downloaded software updates overnight. When enabled, updates will install while your Tesla is parked and not in use, ensuring you always have the latest features and improvements.
To enable, go to Controls > Software > Automatically Install Updates.

✅ Dashcam Viewer Updates
Recent Dashcam footage can now extend up to 24 hours, and you can save any clip for permanent storage on your Tesla or on your phone by going to App Launcher > Dashcam Viewer.
Clips downloaded to your phone now include details such as speed, steering wheel angle, and Self-Driving state.

✅ Weather Maps Improvements
Weather maps now show snow and rain with improved colors that make it easier to distinguish between precipitation types. You can also view the past hour of weather data to see how conditions have been changing along your route.

✅ Supercharger Pricing Filter
You can now choose to route only to Superchargers with Tesla pricing. When enabled, Trip Planner will avoid locations where Tesla does not set charging prices or accept Free Supercharging credits.

✅ Keyboard
Your selected keyboard language will also select the language used for dictation. Tap the international keyboard button to change keyboard and dictation language, without changing voice command language. Tap the mic icon in the keyboard to dictate.

✅ Rear Display
Passengers in rear seats can now view and interact with maps on the rear display while your Tesla navigates a route. Tap the maps icon from the rear display to follow along.

✅ Security Improvements

✅ Larger icons on the bottom bar [undocumented]

✅ Animated badge in software menu [undocumented]

✅ Audio source icon next to the volume slider [undocumented]

quoted_tweet.text:
🚨 테슬라 옵티머스 Gen 3, “진짜 양산형 로봇”  

1️⃣ 손 기술, 인간 수준으로 점프
Gen 3의 핵심은 손입니다.
22 자유도(DoF)로 기존 대비 2배 수준의 정밀 제어가 가능해졌고, 힘줄 구조를 모방한 tendon-driven 설계와 촉각 센서까지 결합되며 단순 집기가 아닌 “조작” 영역으로 넘어왔습니다.
→ 달걀을 깨지 않게 잡고, 신발끈을 묶는 수준의 정밀 작업 가능

2️⃣ 액추에이터 구조 혁신
손에 직접 모터를 넣는 대신, 팔뚝에 50개 액추에이터를 집중 배치하는 구조로 변경
→ 무게 감소 + 발열 관리 + 유지보수 효율까지 동시에 개선

3️⃣ 스펙은 “사람과 거의 동일한 작업 단위”
• 키 173cm / 무게 57kg
• 배터리 2.33kWh → 58시간 이상 작동
• 페이로드 20~40kg
• 최고 속도 최대 20km/h 수준
→ 공장 노동 기준에서 “실제 대체 가능한 수준”에 근접

4️⃣ AI는 사실상 FSD의 확장판
AI5 칩 + FSD v15 기반 End-to-End 구조
→ 카메라 8개로 환경을 실시간 3D 인식
→ 사람이 시범을 보이면 그대로 학습 (Imitation Learning)
→ 작업을 반복할수록 성능이 개선되는 구조

5️⃣ Grok 음성 AI 통합 (xAI)
자연어 이해 + 음성 명령 기반 작업 수행
→ “가서 저거 가져와” 같은 인간형 인터페이스 가능성

6️⃣ 설계 철학이 완전히 바뀜
Gen 2 = 기술 데모
Gen 3 = 처음부터 “대량 생산용” 설계
→ 모듈화 + 단순화 + 생산성 중심 구조
→ 연간 100만 대 생산 라인 목표

7️⃣ 디자인 방향성 변화
“로봇처럼 생긴 기계” → “사람처럼 보이는 기계”
외형은 미니멀하고 매끄럽게, 내부는 극도로 공학적으로 최적화

8️⃣ 현재 진행 상황
2026년 7~8월 프리몬트 공장에서 저속 생산(LRIP) 시작 예정
이미 내부 테스트 및 특허에서 손 성능은 상당 부분 검증된 상태

> 핵심 
Gen 3는 단순 업그레이드가 아니라, “실제 노동 투입이 가능한 첫 번째 로봇” 단계로 넘어온 모델입니다.

kept.text:
EVC TBX Pro — Tesla Diagnostic Tool for Professional Workshops
Stop fighting Tesla Toolbox 3. Start fixing cars.
EVC TBX Pro is a professional diagnostic layer built on top of Tesla Toolbox 3, designed by EV Clinic R&D for workshops that service Tesla vehicles every day. It eliminates the connection failures, timeouts and dead-ends that turn a 30-second routine into a full day of frustration.

What you get
•Pre-ODIN connection layer that bypasses the failure points where TBX3 gets stuck on “Connect to proxy” or “Seceth unlock.” Connection in seconds, not hours.
•Reliable connection to Model S/X with NVIDIA Tegra infotainment — no more “reboot the MCU” or “factory reset and try again” forum folklore that never actually works.
•Hidden service functions exposed in a clean interface, accessible for daily workshop use.
•Full Vehicle Health Report — complete vehicle certificate with battery SOH, capacity, cell balance, range estimate, all vitals, active and recent alerts, and configuration. Exports as a PDF with QR code and certificate ID. Perfect for certified used EV sales — no hidden battery problems, no surprises for the buyer.
•GTW coding directly from the tool.
•Full DTC diagnostics with descriptions, customer text and additional info — not just raw codes.
•Full system search across 350+ tasks in 25 categories. Searchable by name, description or parameter.
•Advanced data access for technicians who need to go beyond standard routines.
Requirements
EVC TBX Pro is an add-on layer. To use it, your workshop must have:
•A valid Tesla Toolbox 3 account with active subscription (purchased separately from Tesla)
•Standard Tesla diagnostic hardware
•Windows PC
Pro does not replace Tesla’s subscription — it makes it actually usable.
Demo / Trial
A 2-month demo early access license is available for €120 + VAT, giving your workshop full access to evaluate EVC TBX Pro on real vehicles before committing to a full subscription. After the demo period you can upgrade to the full Pro license seamlessly. Only 50 dev accounts will be activated starting 8.5.2026 - now preorders are active.

Support
Installation, onboarding and technical support provided directly by EV Clinic R&D. Franchise partners receive priority support and integration with the EVC ecosystem (EVC CMR, EVC Academy, Vehicle Health Report). For others, we will need case by case feedback for potential bugs.

Soon

Habrá una burbuja de la IA. Por supuesto, ha ocurrido con todas las grandes revoluciones tecnológicas. Al final la avaricia y los excesos llevan a situaciones surrealistas. 

Probablemente se genere en las salidas a bolsa de los nuevos jugadores, si se calientan demasiado. 

Pero no la hay en los grandes  jugadores actuales. Con niveles de PER que nada tienen que ver con los de la burbuja del año 2000 (excepto Tesla Y es un caso muy particular) y que, en base a los últimos resultados publicados, solo están ligeramente por encima de la media histórica del SP 500. Con beneficios que crecen al triple o al cuádruple de la media de crecimiento de beneficios de dicho índice, a día de hoy no hay tal burbuja.

kept.text:
AMAZING JOURNEY 🇨🇳 @Tesla HAS BEEN IN CHINA FOR 12 YEARS 🔥

From the launch of the first Tesla Supercharger in mainland China at Shanghai Jinqiao in 2014, to the establishment of Giga Shanghai, the R&D Innovation Center, and the Shanghai Megafactory—and as Tesla community of owners continues to grow—the past 12 years have been a testament to "Tesla Speed."

Here’s a quick recap of all remarkable milestones of Tesla China 🔥

🇨🇳 April 2014: Tesla officially enters the Chinese market, delivering the first batch of Model S vehicles and inaugurating mainland China's first Supercharger station at Shanghai Jinqiao.

🏗️ January 2019: Groundbreaking takes place for Giga Shanghai, Tesla’s first Gigafactory outside the United States.

🚗 December 2019: The first "Made-in-China" Model 3 vehicles roll off the line and are delivered, achieving start-of-production less than a year after groundbreaking.

⚙️ February 2021: The Shanghai Supercharger factory is completed and officially put into production.

🧠 October 2021: Tesla officially opens its R&D Innovation Center and Gigafactory Data Center in Shanghai, localizing its engineering, vehicle development, and data storage.

🏆 August 2022: Giga Shanghai achieves its first major manufacturing milestone, producing its 1 millionth vehicle.

📈 September 2023: Production at Giga Shanghai officially crosses the 2 million vehicle mark.

🔋 May 2024: Tesla breaks ground on the Shanghai Megafactory, its first dedicated commercial energy storage manufacturing project outside the U.S., designed to produce up to 10,000 Megapacks annually.

🛠️ August 2024: The 10 millionth global electric drive system officially rolls off the assembly line at Giga Shanghai.

🚀 October 2024: Giga Shanghai achieves the 3 million vehicle production milestone.

🏗️ December 2024: Construction of the Shanghai Megafactory is successfully completed. Taking just 7 months from its groundbreaking in May, this rapid build once again refreshed the standard for "Tesla Speed."

⚡ Q1 2025: The Shanghai Megafactory commences initial production, following the completion of construction at the end of 2024.

🏁 December 2025: Giga Shanghai rapidly produces its 4 millionth vehicle—scaling from 3 to 4 million units in under 14 months—and closes out the year by manufacturing Tesla's 9 millionth global EV.

⏱️ Unmatched efficiency: Giga Shanghai's component localization rate now exceeds 95%, allowing a new pure electric vehicle to roll off the line every 30-plus seconds on average.

🌐 Global hub: Giga Shanghai has evolved into Tesla's most important global export center, with vehicles shipping to over 30 countries and markets worldwide.

kept.text:
Tesla has just unveiled a new charger for the Tesla Semi called Basecharger, which is designed for overnight charging of Tesla Semis.

• 125 kW charging speed
• Adds up to 60% of range in four hours
• Deliveries begin in early 2027
• "Home charging" for heavy-duty fleets
• 180–1000 VDC
• Cable length: 6 meters
• Continuous output: 150 A
• Size and weight: H x W x D: 340 x 1200 x 2000 mm, 100 kg
• Fully integrated design that eliminates the need for a separate AC-to-DC cabinet, simplifying installation. The 6 meter cable offers layout flexibility for all depots.
• Supports open protocols, ISO15118-2, OCPI capable
• Charging standard: MCS 3.2

Tesla: "Up to 3 Basechargers can be daisy-chained on a single breaker (sharing 125 kVA) to further reduce installation and operational costs."

I think a lot of businesses are going to take advantage of this, as it will reduce overall costs for them, especially due to cheaper nighttime electricity rates.

• 125 kW charging speed
• Adds up to 60% of range in four hours
• Deliveries begin in early 2027
• "Home charging" for heavy-duty fleets
• 180–1000 VDC
• Cable length: 6 meters
• Continuous output: 150 A
• Size and weight: H x W x D: 340 x 1200 x 2000 mm, 100 kg
• Fully integrated design that eliminates the need for a separate AC-to-DC cabinet, simplifying installation. The 6 meter cable offers layout flexibility for all depots.
• Supports open protocols, ISO15118-2, OCPI capable
• Charging standard: MCS 3.2

Tesla: "Up to 3 Basechargers can be daisy-chained on a single breaker (sharing 125 kVA) to further reduce installation and operational costs."

I think a lot of businesses are going to take advantage of this, as it will reduce overall costs for them, especially due to cheaper nighttime electricity rates.

ABD'nin devasa borç yükünü çevirebilmek için faizleri indirmek zorunda olduğunu, enflasyon verilerinin ise medyan çekirdek gibi yeni yöntemlerle daha düşük gösterilerek bu indirime kılıf hazırlanacağını
Şahin açıklamalara rağmen piyasaların (altın, gümüş ve endeksler) yukarı yönlü tepki vermesini, Fed'in aslında faiz artırmayacağına dair bir güven göstergesi olarak yorumluyor.

⛽ Enerji ve Petrol 
Alternatif boru hatları sayesinde, BAE'nin OPEC'ten ayrılması demek günde 1,5 milyon varil ek petrol basması demek. Suudi Arabistan ya pazar payı kaybetmeyi göze alacakk ya da daha fazla petrol basacak bu da petrol fiyatlarının hızla 75-85 dolar bandına gerileyebileceğini düşünüyor.

📈 Hisse Senedi
#INTC: Intel'i "İYT'de yaşa takılmayanlara" benzeterek gülüyor. 3'te 1'ini realize ederim diyor. 3'te 1'inin stopunu 80 dolara diğerini 70 veya 75 dolara çekerim diyor. Bütün haberlerin fiyatlandığını söylüyor. 
#TSLA: Kısa vadeli dalgalanmalara rağmen Tesla'nın önce 700, ardından 1.000 dolar seviyelerine ulaşabileceğini öngörüyor. Robot (Optimus) tarafındaki gelişmelerin uzun vadede asıl hikaye olduğunu savunuyor. #ıntc'yi kaçırdıysanız Tesla'yı düşünebilirsiniz diyor.
#QXO: Beğeniyor. 40 dolara gitme potansiyeli görüyor ve mevcut seviyeleri (20 dolar bandı) alım fırsatı olarak değerlendiriyor.
#ORCL: Yapay zeka ve veri merkezi yatırımları nedeniyle uzun vadede çok iyi performans bekliyor.
#SOFI: Uzun vadede iyi olmaya devam eder ancak şu anda bir pozisyonu yok zamanı gelince yeniden girerim diyor. Buradan kimseye girin diyemem uçtu gitti diyor.
#MRN: Elektrik alt yapısını yapan şirket. Şebekelere ekipman satıyor. Fransız şirket. Şu an 30 Euro'da sabrederseniz 50'ye gider diyor.

🇹🇷Borsa İstanbul
Türkiye ekonomisi ve borsasının bu yıl olağanüstü bir performans sergileyeceğini, özellikle bankacılık ve sanayi hisselerinin ön planda kalacağını düşünüyor.
#THYAO ve #SAHOL Beğeniyorum diyor.

🏅 Değerli Metaller
Dolar endeksinin zayıflayacağı ve faiz indirimlerinin geleceği bir senaryoda altın ve gümüşün çok güçlü bir yükseliş yaşayacağını tahmin ediyor.
